subject: SCIENCE
title: Plants Help Life
objective: Explain ways plants support living systems.
introduction: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Plants Help Life”.
A key word used in the lesson “Plants Help Life”.
A key word used in the lesson “Plants Help Life”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Every plant is safe to eat

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: What do plants generally make through photosynthesis?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. soil and plastic do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Plants Help Life”?

options.text: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.
Every plant is safe to eat

items.explanation: The correct response is “Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.

options.text: Every plant is safe to eat

items.explanation: The correct response is “trees provide shade” because it follows the lesson explanation: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Plants eat soil” because it follows the lesson explanation: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.

items.prompt: What do plants generally make through photosynthesis?
items.explanation: The correct response is “sugars” because it follows the lesson explanation: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “many foods come from plants” because it follows the lesson explanation: Plants make sugars through photosynthesis and can provide food, oxygen, shade, habitat, and materials.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Plants Help Life; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Plants Help Life.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
